manifest.description: Canonical mapping of SQL Sentry condition names to Redgate Monitor alert type IDs, with severity consolidation and migration strategy notes.

severityMapping.strategy: SQL Sentry uses an integer scale (0–3) internally. Redgate Monitor exposes three named threshold bands rendered as Blue (Low), Amber (Medium), and Red (High). Severity 0 is informational and collapses to Low; severity 1 remains Low; severity 2 maps to Medium; severity 3 maps to High. Advisory best-practice conditions that are not real-time performance emergencies are intentionally downgraded one level from their SQL Sentry default so that Red bandwidth is reserved for active incidents.
severityMapping.advisoryDowngradeRule: Advisory / best-practice conditions (index health, configuration drift, VLF count, compatibility level) default to Low even when SQL Sentry marks them Medium. Escalate only when the condition has persisted beyond an agreed remediation window.

phases.name: Direct Replacements
phases.description: Conditions with a 1-to-1 built-in Redgate Monitor alert type. Enable immediately — no custom metric setup required.

phases.name: Partial / Configured Replacements
phases.description: Built-in Redgate Monitor alerts that cover the spirit of the SQL Sentry condition but require threshold tuning or supplemental Extended Events / error log monitoring.
conditions: High Percentage CPU for Single Core

phases.name: Custom Metrics
phases.description: No built-in Redgate Monitor equivalent. Build a Custom Metric using the provided T-SQL query for each condition. Group related DMV queries into a single custom metric where possible to reduce alert noise.

conditions: High Average Wait Time Per User Session
SQL Server Process Paged to Disk
High Ad Hoc Query Plans in Cache
Optimize For Ad Hoc Workloads Changed
Sleeping Session With Long Open Transaction

phases.name: Not Available — Decommission or Replace
phases.description: Conditions that require VMware/Hyper-V inventory, WMI, or SQL Sentry internal state. Cannot be replicated in Redgate Monitor. Recommend alternative monitoring tools or accept the gap.

alternatives.condition: Availability Replicas Hosted on Same Virtual Host
alternatives.alternative: Use VMware vRealize or Hyper-V SCVMM placement policies.

alternatives.condition: Availability Replicas With Disks in Same Datastore
alternatives.alternative: Use VMware vRealize datastore anti-affinity rules.

alternatives.condition: High Performance Power Plan Not Enabled
alternatives.alternative: Enforce via Group Policy (PowerCfg) or DSC on all SQL Server hosts.
Redgate Monitor has built-in agent health monitoring — no equivalent condition needed.

conditions.migrationNote: Machine-level CPU threshold. Enable in Redgate Monitor > Alert Settings. Recommended threshold: Low=70%, Medium=85%, High=95%.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Use the built-in alert. Disable the SQL Sentry condition after RG alert is validated.

conditions.migrationNote: Machine-level available RAM threshold. Maps directly to Redgate Monitor 'OS memory available' alert.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement. Set thresholds to match your SQL Server max memory headroom.

conditions.migrationNote: Latency benchmarks: <10ms=fast, 10–20ms=acceptable, 20–50ms=slow, >50ms=critical. Transaction logs should stay under 2ms.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement. Tune Low/Medium/High thresholds to match your storage tier SLAs.

conditions.migrationNote: Disabled by default in Redgate Monitor v14+. Enable under Alert Settings > Resources > Network.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement once the alert is enabled.

conditions.migrationNote: Instance connectivity and service check. Built in to Redgate Monitor — always enabled.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement.

conditions.migrationNote: Head-blocker wait time threshold. Redgate Monitor also surfaces a live blocking tree.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement. Recommend Low=30s, Medium=60s, High=120s.

conditions.migrationNote: Event alert with deadlock graph captured via Extended Events session.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement. Redgate Monitor's deadlock alert captures the full deadlock graph.

conditions.migrationNote: Based on msdb.dbo.backupset backup_finish_date vs configured interval.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement. Configure separate thresholds for FULL, DIFF, and LOG backup intervals.

conditions.migrationNote: Reads msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory for run_status = 0 (failed).
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement.

conditions.migrationNote: Machine-level Disk Used % or GB free threshold.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement. Set both a % and an absolute GB threshold so large drives still alert.

conditions.ssConditionName: AG / Cluster Failover / Mirroring State Change
conditions.migrationNote: Event alert for HA topology changes — failover, role change, replica state.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement.

conditions.migrationNote: Elapsed time threshold. Redgate Monitor default is 90s. Tune per workload.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement.

conditions.migrationNote: Open transactions block log truncation, grow tempdb under RCSI, and escalate locks. Also enable the Blocking processes alert for full coverage.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Map to Long running query. Pair with Blocking processes alert for context.

conditions.migrationNote: Monitor SQL error log for 'Login failed' messages. Supplement with Extended Events audit session for detailed attribution.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Enable SQL Server log entry alert with keyword filter 'Login failed'.

conditions.migrationNote: Configuration change detection. Log entry alert or supplemental Extended Events. Recommended baseline: MAXDOP=logical_cores/2 (max 8), CTP=50.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Use SQL Server log entry alert or consolidate into the Configuration Drift custom metric group.

conditions.ssConditionName: Network Bottleneck — Outbound Output Queue Length
conditions.migrationNote: Output Queue Length > 3 requires WMI/OS-level monitoring. Redgate Monitor Network utilization is the closest built-in equivalent.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Use Network utilization alert as proxy. For precise output queue length alerting, use OS monitoring (PRTG, Datadog, etc.).

conditions.ssConditionName: High Percentage CPU for Single Core Saturation
conditions.migrationNote: Redgate Monitor alerts on overall CPU%. Per-core saturation proxy: threshold = 100 / logical_core_count. E.g. on a 16-core server, one saturated core = ~6% total.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Use CPU utilization alert with a low-% threshold as a proxy. Per-core granularity requires OS-level monitoring.

conditions.ssConditionName: Availability Replicas Have Send or Recovery Queue Exceeding Threshold
conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT MAX(CASE WHEN send_queue_size > 3072 OR redo_queue_size > 3072 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS queue_breached FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states WHERE is_local = 0
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when result = 1 (3 MB / 3072 KB default)
conditions.migrationNote: Part of Scheduler Health consolidation group. Alert when either send or redo queue exceeds 3 MB.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate with Mirroring Queue metric into a single 'Replication Queue' custom metric.

conditions.ssConditionName: Mirrored Databases Have Send or Recovery Queue Exceeding Threshold
conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT MAX(CASE WHEN mirroring_send_queue_size > 3072 OR mirroring_redo_queue_size > 3072 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS queue_breached FROM sys.database_mirroring WHERE mirroring_guid IS NOT NULL
conditions.migrationNote: Mirror is deprecated in SQL 2022. Prioritise AG queue metric if on modern SQL Server.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate with AG Replica Queue into a single 'Replication Queue' custom metric group.

conditions.ssConditionName: Availability Replicas Hosted on Same Virtual Host
conditions.migrationNote: Requires VMware/Hyper-V host inventory API — no T-SQL equivalent.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Use VMware vRealize anti-affinity rules or Hyper-V SCVMM placement policies instead.

conditions.ssConditionName: Availability Replicas With Disks in Same Datastore
conditions.migrationNote: Requires VMware datastore inventory — no T-SQL equivalent.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Enforce via VMware datastore anti-affinity rules. Accept the monitoring gap in Redgate Monitor.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS untrusted_checks FROM sys.check_constraints WHERE is_not_trusted = 1 AND is_disabled = 0
conditions.migrationNote: Untrusted constraints prevent the query optimiser from using constraint elimination. Run WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT ALL on offending tables.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Index / Constraint Health' custom metric group. Low severity — best practice, not an emergency.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS untrusted_fks FROM sys.foreign_keys WHERE is_not_trusted = 1 AND is_disabled = 0
conditions.migrationNote: Untrusted FKs are usually caused by bulk inserts with NOCHECK or restores. Run ALTER TABLE ... WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT to re-trust.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Index / Constraint Health' custom metric group.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS non_checksum_dbs FROM sys.databases WHERE page_verify_option_desc <> 'CHECKSUM' AND state_desc = 'ONLINE' AND name NOT IN ('tempdb')
conditions.migrationNote: CHECKSUM is the only page verification option that detects I/O corruption. TORN_PAGE_DETECTION catches only partial writes.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Index / Constraint Health' custom metric group. Medium severity due to data integrity risk.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT CAST(value_in_use AS INT) AS current_ctp FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'cost threshold for parallelism'
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when value differs from your agreed baseline (recommended baseline: 50)
conditions.migrationNote: Store the expected value in the threshold. Alert on deviation rather than an absolute value.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate all three configuration change conditions (CTP, MAXDOP, ad hoc workloads) into a single 'Configuration Drift' custom metric.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT CAST(value_in_use AS INT) AS current_maxdop FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'max degree of parallelism'
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when value differs from your agreed baseline
conditions.migrationNote: Recommended: MAXDOP = min(logical_cores / 2, 8) for OLTP. Alert on any deviation.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Configuration Drift' custom metric group.

conditions.ssConditionName: Optimize for Ad Hoc Workloads Changed
conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT CAST(value_in_use AS INT) AS ad_hoc_enabled FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'optimize for ad hoc workloads'
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when = 0 (should always be enabled on OLTP servers)
conditions.migrationNote: Should be enabled on all OLTP instances to avoid single-use plan cache bloat.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Configuration Drift' custom metric group.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS failed_workers FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WHERE failed_to_create_worker = 1
conditions.migrationNote: Typically caused by memory pressure preventing new worker thread creation. Immediate action required.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Part of 'Scheduler Health' consolidation group. Keep as High severity — this is a critical condition.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS hot_added FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WHERE status LIKE '%HOT ADDED%'
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when > 0 (informational — CPU was added dynamically)
conditions.migrationNote: Detects dynamic CPU expansion. Informational — validate that SQL Server is correctly using the new scheduler.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Scheduler Health' custom metric group. Low severity.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS visible_offline FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WHERE is_online = 0 AND scheduler_id < 255 AND status NOT LIKE '%HIDDEN%'
conditions.migrationNote: Offline CPUs cause NUMA imbalance and licensing waste. Check affinity mask settings.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Scheduler Health' custom metric group.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS unused_schedulers FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WHERE is_online = 0 AND scheduler_id < 255
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when > 0 (review if intentional via affinity mask)
conditions.migrationNote: Caused by affinity masking or licensing restrictions. Validate that offline schedulers are intentional.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Scheduler Health' custom metric group.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(signal_wait_time_ms) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(SUM(wait_time_ms), 0) * 100 AS signal_wait_pct FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN ('SLEEP_TASK','SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH','WAITFOR','BROKER_TO_FLUSH','BROKER_EVENTHANDLER','CHECKPOINT_QUEUE','DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE','SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP','SLEEP_DBSTARTUP','SLEEP_DBRECOVER','SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY','SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY','SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED','SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP','SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK','SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP','SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT','SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP','SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH','WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG')
conditions.migrationNote: High signal waits indicate CPU pressure — tasks are ready to run but waiting for a scheduler. Correlates with runnable task count.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Wait Analysis' custom metric group.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT c.cntr_value AS compilations_per_sec, b.cntr_value AS batches_per_sec, CASE WHEN b.cntr_value > 0 THEN CAST(c.cntr_value AS FLOAT)/b.cntr_value*100 ELSE 0 END AS compile_ratio_pct FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters c CROSS JOIN sys.dm_os_performance_counters b WHERE c.counter_name = 'SQL Compilations/sec' AND c.object_name LIKE '%SQL Statistics%' AND b.counter_name = 'Batch Requests/sec' AND b.object_name LIKE '%SQL Statistics%'
conditions.migrationNote: High compile ratio indicates poor plan reuse — check for parameter sniffing, missing sp_executesql, or frequent ad-hoc SQL.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Compilation Rates' custom metric group with recompilations.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT cntr_value AS recompilations_per_sec FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE counter_name = 'SQL Re-Compilations/sec' AND object_name LIKE '%SQL Statistics%'
conditions.migrationNote: Frequent recompilations are caused by schema changes, SET option changes, or statistics updates mid-execution.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Compilation Rates' custom metric group.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT AVG(runnable_tasks_count) AS avg_runnable FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WHERE scheduler_id < 255 AND is_online = 1
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when avg > 10 (strong CPU pressure indicator)
conditions.migrationNote: avg_runnable_tasks > 10 sustained is a reliable indicator of CPU saturation. Correlates with Signal Wait % alert.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Scheduler Health' custom metric group. Keep High severity.

conditions.ssConditionName: High Percentage CPU for Non-SQL Server Process
conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT 100 - sqlcpu.cntr_value AS non_sql_cpu_approx FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters sqlcpu WHERE sqlcpu.counter_name = 'SQL Server:CPU Usage %' AND sqlcpu.object_name LIKE '%Resource Pool Stats%' AND sqlcpu.instance_name = 'default'
conditions.migrationNote: Full per-process CPU attribution requires WMI. T-SQL proxy compares SQL CPU share vs total. Use machine-level CPU% vs SQL CPU% as the indicator.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Scheduler Health' group or use as a supplemental OS alert. Medium severity.

conditions.migrationNote: Maps directly to Redgate Monitor avg read-write time alert. Benchmarks: <10ms fast, 10–20ms acceptable, 20–50ms slow, >50ms critical.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Direct replacement. Set Low=20ms, Medium=35ms, High=50ms as starting thresholds.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT SUM(pending_disk_io_count) AS total_pending_io FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WHERE scheduler_id < 255
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when > 0 sustained (any pending I/O indicates I/O subsystem saturation)
conditions.migrationNote: Sustained pending I/O is a critical indicator — the I/O subsystem cannot keep up with demand.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Standalone metric. High severity. Pair with avg read-write time alert for full I/O picture.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT DB_NAME() AS db_name, COUNT(*) AS vlf_count FROM sys.dm_db_log_info(DB_ID()) HAVING COUNT(*) > 1000
conditions.migrationNote: High VLF count slows log backups and recovery. Fix: backup the log, shrink to minimum, then resize with one large growth increment (e.g. 4 GB in a single step).
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'File & Storage' custom metric group. Low severity — maintenance issue, not a real-time emergency.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT name, DB_NAME(database_id) AS db_name, size * 8 / 1024 AS size_mb FROM sys.master_files
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when size_mb decreases vs prior snapshot (compare current vs stored baseline)
conditions.migrationNote: Data file shrinks cause index fragmentation and log file VLF proliferation. Almost always harmful — investigate the root cause immediately.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'File & Storage' custom metric group. High severity — shrink events are always actionable.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT DB_NAME(database_id) AS db_name, type_desc, COUNT(*) AS file_count FROM sys.master_files GROUP BY database_id, type_desc
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when count differs from stored baseline
conditions.migrationNote: Unexpected file count changes should trigger a backup job review. New files added outside change control are a DBA concern.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'File & Storage' custom metric group. Low severity — informational.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT name, DB_NAME(database_id) AS db_name, physical_name FROM sys.master_files WHERE is_percent_growth = 0 AND growth = 0
conditions.migrationNote: Disabled auto-growth with no fixed growth means DML will halt when space is exhausted. Enable auto-growth as a safety net (even if you pre-size files).
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'File & Storage' custom metric group. High severity — can cause complete DML halt.

conditions.ssConditionName: Auto-Growth Next Event Exceeds Free Disk Space
conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT name, DB_NAME(database_id) AS db_name, (max_size - size - growth) * 8 / 1024 AS space_debt_mb FROM sys.master_files WHERE growth > 0 AND max_size > 0 AND (max_size - size) < growth
conditions.migrationNote: The next auto-growth event would exceed available disk space. Pre-empt by adding disk space or reducing the growth increment.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'File & Storage' custom metric group. High severity — pre-emptive disk space alert.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT cntr_value AS ple FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE counter_name = 'Page life expectancy' AND object_name LIKE '%Buffer Manager%'
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when PLE < (buffer_pool_GB / 4) * 300. E.g. 16 GB buffer pool = alert at < 1200s
conditions.migrationNote: PLE dropping rapidly indicates buffer pool churn. Check per NUMA node via '%Buffer Node%' object_name for multi-NUMA servers.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Memory Pressure' custom metric group. High severity.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT cntr_value AS memory_grants_pending FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE counter_name = 'Memory Grants Pending' AND object_name LIKE '%Memory Manager%'
conditions.migrationNote: Any pending memory grants indicate RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE waits. Correlates directly with query throughput degradation.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Memory Pressure' custom metric group. High severity — alert threshold is > 0.

conditions.ssConditionName: SQL Server Process Paged to Disk
conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT physical_memory_in_use_kb, committed_target_kb, CAST(physical_memory_in_use_kb AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(committed_target_kb, 0) AS memory_ratio FROM sys.dm_os_process_memory
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when memory_ratio < 0.75 (SQL Server using less than 75% of its committed target — indicates OS paging)
conditions.migrationNote: SQL Server being paged to disk is a critical performance emergency. Lock pages in memory (LPIM) should be enabled on production SQL Servers.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Memory Pressure' custom metric group. High severity — critical condition.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT system_cache_kb, available_physical_memory_kb, total_physical_memory_kb, CAST(system_cache_kb AS FLOAT)/total_physical_memory_kb*100 AS cache_pct FROM sys.dm_os_sys_memory
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when cache_pct > 50% AND available_physical_memory_kb < 524288 (< 512 MB free)
conditions.migrationNote: Critical in SSAS or mixed-workload environments. Windows file cache can starve SQL Server of memory if LPIM is not enabled.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Memory Pressure' custom metric group. Medium severity.

conditions.ssConditionName: High Ad Hoc Query Plans in Cache
conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) * 8 / 1024 AS adhoc_cache_mb, (SELECT SUM(size_in_bytes)/1024/1024 FROM sys.dm_exec_cached_plans) AS total_cache_mb FROM sys.dm_exec_cached_plans WHERE usecounts = 1 AND objtype = 'Adhoc'
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when adhoc_cache_mb > 10% of total cache. For servers with >64 GB RAM use 5%.
conditions.migrationNote: Fix by enabling 'optimize for ad hoc workloads'. Single-use plans bloat the plan cache and cause PLE drops under memory pressure.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Memory Pressure' custom metric group. Pair with 'Optimize for Ad Hoc Workloads' configuration check.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT AVG(work_queue_count) AS avg_work_queue FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WHERE scheduler_id < 255 AND is_online = 1
conditions.migrationNote: Work queue > 1 means threads are waiting for available workers. Investigate max worker threads setting.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Scheduler Health' custom metric group.

conditions.ssConditionName: High Average Wait Time Per User Session
conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT SUM(r.wait_time) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS avg_wait_ms_per_session FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions s JOIN sys.dm_exec_requests r ON s.session_id = r.session_id WHERE s.is_user_process = 1
conditions.migrationNote: Sustained high average wait per user session indicates system-wide resource contention. Correlate with wait type breakdown.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Wait Analysis' custom metric group.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS active_user_sessions FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE is_user_process = 1 AND last_request_start_time > DATEADD(MINUTE, -15, GETDATE())
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Set threshold to match your workload baseline (high session count alone does not indicate a problem)
conditions.migrationNote: Informational — use as a capacity planning indicator rather than a reactive alert.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Session Activity' custom metric group. Low severity.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS activation_errors FROM sys.dm_broker_activated_tasks WHERE spid IS NULL
conditions.migrationNote: Activation errors mean the stored procedure triggered by the queue is failing to start.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate all 6 Service Broker conditions into a single 'Service Broker Health' custom metric.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS login_errors FROM sys.dm_broker_connections WHERE login_state = 13
conditions.migrationNote: Login state 13 indicates a transport-layer authentication failure in the Service Broker endpoint.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Service Broker Health' custom metric group. High severity.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS queues_without_poison_handling FROM sys.service_queues WHERE is_poison_message_handling_enabled = 0
conditions.migrationNote: A poison message can halt all queue processing indefinitely. Poison handling must always be enabled.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Service Broker Health' custom metric group. High severity.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS queues_with_issues FROM sys.service_queues WHERE activation_procedure IS NOT NULL AND (is_activation_enabled = 0 OR is_receive_enabled = 0 OR is_enqueue_enabled = 0)
conditions.migrationNote: Queue with activation procedure but disabled receive/enqueue will silently drop messages.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Service Broker Health' custom metric group.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT cntr_value AS task_limit_reached_per_sec FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE counter_name = 'Task Limit Reached/sec' AND object_name LIKE '%Broker Activation%'
conditions.migrationNote: Task limit saturation means the MAX_QUEUE_READERS setting is too low for the message volume.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Service Broker Health' custom metric group.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS transmission_errors FROM sys.transmission_queue WHERE is_conversation_error = 1
conditions.migrationNote: Transmission queue errors indicate Service Broker conversation failures — messages are stranded.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Service Broker Health' custom metric group.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT CAST(value_in_use AS INT) AS common_criteria_enabled FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'common criteria compliance enabled'
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when = 1 (should be 0 unless mandated by compliance)
conditions.migrationNote: Enabling Common Criteria Compliance can dramatically degrade performance on Enterprise/Datacenter due to last successful login tracking on every connection.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Configuration Drift' custom metric group. Low severity.

conditions.ssConditionName: High Performance Power Plan Not Enabled
conditions.migrationNote: Requires WMI to read Windows Power Plan — no T-SQL equivalent.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Enforce via Group Policy (PowerCfg /setactive SCHEME_MIN) applied to all SQL Server OU members. Accept this monitoring gap in Redgate Monitor.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT COUNT(*) AS numa_255_schedulers FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WHERE numa_node_id = 255 AND scheduler_id < 255
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when > 0 on NUMA-capable hardware (numa_node_id 255 = soft-NUMA disabled)
conditions.migrationNote: Soft-NUMA improves memory and I/O affinity on multi-socket systems. Enable via ALTER SERVER CONFIGURATION SET SOFTNUMA ON.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Configuration Drift' custom metric group. Low severity — best practice, not an emergency.

conditions.migrationNote: SQL Sentry internal service health check — not applicable in Redgate Monitor.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Redgate Monitor has built-in agent health monitoring. No equivalent condition required.

conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT name, compatibility_level FROM sys.databases WHERE compatibility_level < (SELECT compatibility_level FROM sys.databases WHERE name = 'model') AND state_desc = 'ONLINE' AND name NOT IN ('master','msdb','tempdb')
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when any row returned (review intentional exceptions)
conditions.migrationNote: Databases running at an old compat level miss cardinality estimator improvements. Exceptions for legacy applications should be documented and acknowledged.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Configuration Drift' custom metric group. Low severity.

conditions.ssConditionName: Sleeping Session With Long Open Transaction
conditions.customMetricSQL: SELECT s.session_id, s.host_name, s.program_name, t.transaction_begin_time, DATEDIFF(MINUTE, t.transaction_begin_time, GETDATE()) AS open_minutes FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions s JOIN sys.dm_tran_session_transactions st ON s.session_id = st.session_id JOIN sys.dm_tran_active_transactions t ON st.transaction_id = t.transaction_id WHERE s.status = 'sleeping' AND DATEDIFF(MINUTE, t.transaction_begin_time, GETDATE()) >= 10
conditions.customMetricThreshold: Alert when any row returned (sleeping with open transaction for >= 10 minutes)
conditions.migrationNote: Sleeping sessions with open transactions prevent log truncation and cause version store bloat under RCSI/snapshot isolation.
conditions.simplificationStrategy: Consolidate into 'Session Activity' custom metric group. Medium severity.
